senses.definition: plural of racetrack, a prepared oval or circular track for horse or car racing.
examples: The horses thundered around the racetracks during the derby.
Automobile enthusiasts gathered at the NASCAR racetracks this weekend.

senses.definition: a legal term for a person or thing that participates in royalty or racing contexts; appears chiefly as a surname or rare historical term.
examples: The name Racette appears on the historical roster of early racers.
In French, Racette is occasionally used as a surname.

senses.definition: a sport where participants walk as fast as possible with one foot on the ground at all times.
examples: She trained for years to improve her racewalk pace.
Racewalkers competed in the international event.

senses.definition: to walk quickly in a racewalking style, keeping one foot on the ground at all times.
examples: He chose to racewalk the distance to conserve energy.
She racewalked the course during the warm-up.

senses.definition: past tense of racewalk; walked as a racewalker would, with a formal technique.
examples: She racewalked the path to warm up before the race.
He racewalked the distance steadily and won a medal.

senses.definition: a person who participates in racewalking.
examples: The racewalker finished in the top three.
She is a celebrated racewalker in international competitions.

senses.definition: plural of racewalker; multiple athletes who practice racewalking.
examples: Several racewalkers trained together for the world championships.
The event featured many skilled racewalkers.

senses.definition: the act of walking quickly with a racing technique that keeps one foot on the ground at all times.
examples: Racewalking requires strict technique to avoid disqualification.
She has been racewalking for years to improve endurance.

senses.definition: plural form for occurrences or instances of racewalking, rarely used in everyday language.
examples: Historical racewalkings were documented in early sporting records.

senses.definition: plural of racewalk; instances or events of racewalking race.
examples: The racewalks drew large crowds.
Several racewalks were held during the festival.

senses.definition: a track where races are held; a road or passage used by racing vehicles or flowing liquids.
examples: The raceway hosted a short track car race.
Oil flowed along the raceway in the factory.

senses.definition: plural of raceway; multiple racing tracks or passages for racing vehicles or liquids.
examples: Several raceways were built in the new park.
Factory raceways help guide hot oil safely.

senses.definition: a short form or archaic term; may refer to a proto- word or a misspelling; not common in modern usage.
examples: Rach is not commonly used in contemporary English.

senses.definition: rare or dialectal form; may appear as a surname or historical spelling, not common today.
examples: Rache appears in some old texts as a variant spelling.

senses.definition: a personal name; could be a given name (e.g., Rachel).
examples: Rachel won the scholarship.
She introduced herself as Rachel.

senses.definition: plural form of a rarely used name or term; not common in modern usage.
examples: Historical documents mention several raches.

senses.definition: alternative spelling of ratchet; a device that converts rotary motion to linear motion or a slang term for a tool.
examples: The mechanic used a ratchet to loosen the bolt.

senses.definition: past tense of ratchet; to move or cause to be arranged by gradual steps or to tighten with a ratchet.
examples: She racheted the bolt tighter.
The mechanism racheted up over time.

senses.definition: present participle of ratchet; moving in stepped or incremental fashion.
examples: The force racheting the gear system made a clicking sound.

senses.definition: plural of ratchet; tools with a pawl and gear used to tighten or loosen.
examples: He used several ratchets and wrenches.

senses.definition: relating to the spine or spinal region; used in medical terms.
examples: Rachial symptoms include back pain in certain conditions.

senses.definition: a medical condition characterized by pain in the spine.
examples: Rachialgia can be caused by nerve compression.

senses.definition: relating to rachialgia or spinal pain.
examples: Rachialgic symptoms were noted during the exam.

senses.definition: medical term for reduced or absent sensitivity in the spine area; not common in general use.
examples: Rachianalgesia is discussed in specialized neurology texts.

senses.definition: a genus name in paleontology; a taxonomic term referring to a fossil organism with spinal characteristics.
examples: Rachianectes is studied by paleontologists.

senses.definition: medical term for anesthesia of the spine or spinal nerves.
examples: Rachianesthesia may be used in certain surgical procedures.
